meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use project management to overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your career.
content.answer_block: Transform your lack of experience into a professional narrative by building a high-fidelity project management tool. By architecting this system, you demonstrate mastery of database schema design, state management, and real-world utility, turning a blank resume into a showcase of technical engineering competence and product-thinking.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank portfolio is the greatest hurdle a student faces. You have the skills, but the gap between theory and a shippable product feels like a chasm. Employers aren't looking for another 'To-Do list' tutorial; they are hunting for engineers who understand how to structure complex, data-driven systems that solve actual pain points.

Today, you stop being a student and start being an architect. By building a robust project management application, you create a tangible proof of your ability to handle state, user roles, and complex CRUD operations. This project is your gateway to demonstrating the professional rigor that separates a junior coder from a hired engineer.

steps.title: Define the Relational Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema supporting users, projects, tasks, and team relationships. This proves you understand data modeling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema for a project management tool including users, boards, tasks, and task_comments with appropriate foreign key relationships and indexing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create migration files for users, projects, and tasks tables based on the schema provided.'

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Secure your application by defining access levels. This demonstrates security-first engineering.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a middleware structure for RBAC that distinguishes between Admin, Editor, and Viewer roles for project board access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement auth guard middleware to protect task routes based on user role permissions.'

steps.title: Build Real-Time State Management
steps.description: Create a responsive UI that updates state without refreshing. This highlights your frontend proficiency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a React state management pattern using TanStack Query to fetch and mutate tasks in the project management dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor task list component to use TanStack Query for background data synchronization.'

steps.title: Automate Workflow Triggers
steps.description: Add an automation engine that updates task status based on sub-task completion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a business logic function that automatically shifts a parent task to 'In Review' when all sub-tasks are marked 'Done'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a service layer function that triggers task status updates upon sub-task completion.'

steps.title: Deploy with CI/CD Integration
steps.description: Set up a deployment pipeline to showcase your DevOps understanding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs tests and deploys the application to a staging environment on push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure .github/workflows/deploy.yml for automated testing and production deployment.'

pro_tips: Always build for the edge case: assume the user will delete a task while another user is editing it, and handle that conflict.
Document your architectural decisions in a ADR (Architecture Decision Record) file; hiring managers love seeing this professional habit.
Prioritize performance by implementing optimistic UI updates; it makes your app feel instantly responsive.
Use TypeScript strictly; type safety is the single biggest indicator of a developer who writes maintainable enterprise code.
Write unit tests for your business logic first; it forces you to design better APIs before you even write the UI.
content.next_steps: After deployment, add a 'Public Analytics' dashboard to your project to demonstrate data visualization skills, then deploy it to Vercel and include the live link on your resume.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the tools and the strategy to build a project that stands out in a crowded market. Don't just learn—create something that proves your value to future employers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
